title: Ecosystem Services Web
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive vis-network diagram showing the four categories of ecosystem services with interconnections and a Remove Service mode to explore cascading effects.

learningObjectives: Assess the interconnections among the four categories of ecosystem services
Predict consequences of losing specific ecosystem services
Evaluate which ecosystem services are most critical based on connectivity
Explain why Supporting services underpin all other service categories
prerequisites: Understanding of ecosystems and how they function
Introduction to the concept of ecosystem services

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Network visualization with service removal cascading effects
keyQuestions: Which service category, when removed, causes the most widespread cascade?
Why are Supporting services considered the foundation of all ecosystem services?
What happens to food production if pollination is lost?
How does removing a Regulating service affect Provisioning services?
Why should ecosystem services be considered in land development decisions?
commonMisconceptions: Ecosystem services are independent and can be managed separately
Cultural services are less important than Provisioning services
Technology can fully replace lost ecosystem services
assessmentOpportunities: Cascade severity ranking across service categories
Policy recommendation based on service removal analysis
Written argument for why a specific wetland should be preserved
